# GoalOS Mission Autopilot v63

GoalOS Mission Autopilot is the separate high-level public entry console for visitors who want to type one objective and let the site assemble the next proof path.

## What it does

1. Captures a public-safe objective in ordinary language.
2. Builds a local Mission Contract.
3. Selects proof-bearing workstreams.
4. Produces an Evidence Docket outline.
5. Emits a Governed Decision State.
6. Creates an Action Graph with links to existing GoalOS pages.
7. Writes a local Chronicle-style receipt.
8. Exports `GoalOSMissionAutopilotReceipt` JSON.

## What it does not do

No account. No form submission. No public wallet connection. No token approval. No network request from the console. No analytics. No cookies. No browser storage. No transaction broadcast. No production authority. No user data retained.

## Why it exists

Ask GoalOS answers questions. Mission Autopilot is different: it starts from an objective and turns that objective into a proof mission. It is designed for a visitor who says: “Here is what I want; GoalOS should take care of the rest.”
